title: chrysopsis gossypina subsp. gossypina
intro: Chrysopsis gossypina subsp. gossypina, also known as California Bay or Soap Bush, is a shrubby perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Chrysopsis gossypina subsp. gossypina include treating skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
